title: Introduction to altmem
text: Install altmem.

title: Introduction to altmem
text: Load altmem.

title: Introduction to altmem
text: Load example cytoMEM dataset mass cytometry characterization normal human blood. Seven cell populations identified (labels stored cluster column): 1: CD4+ T cells 2: CD8+ T cells 3: dendritic cells (DCs) 4: IgM- B cells 5: IgM+ B cells 6: monocytes 7: natural killer (NK) cells dataset transformed, apply arcsinh transformation cofactor 15.

title: Introduction to altmem
text: Run marker enrichment modeling. Alternatively, transformation can done within MEM function. output list matrices containing MEM scores related statistics. labels calculated, can visualize results.  heatmap can customized: show_mem_labels adds descriptive MEM labels cluster names,max_label_markers limits number markers shown labels, min_heatmap_score adds threshold keeping variable markers.  MEM scores labels agree known biology cell populations. example, cluster 1 (CD4+ T cells) enriched CD4 CD3, cluster 2 (CD8+ T cells) enriched CD8 CD3. labels can extracted simple vector.
code: res <- altmem(PBMCt, cluster_col = "cluster") res <- altmem(PBMC, cluster_col = "cluster", transform = TRUE, cofactor = 15) str(res) #> List of 6 #>  $ MAGpop    :List of 1 #>   ..$ : num [1:7, 1:25] 2.5086 0.0251 0.0287 2.5251 0.0185 ... #>   .. ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:7] "5" "1" "7" "4" ... #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:25] "CD19" "CD117" "CD11b" "CD4" ... #>  $ MAGref    :List of 1 #>   ..$ : num [1:7, 1:25] 0.0231 0.0143 0.0185 0.021 0.0206 ... #>   .. ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:7] "5" "1" "7" "4" ... #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:25] "CD19" "CD117" "CD11b" "CD4" ... #>  $ IQRpop    :List of 1 #>   ..$ : num [1:7, 1:25] 0.649 0.5 0.5 0.641 0.5 ... #>   .. ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:7] "5" "1" "7" "4" ... #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:25] "CD19" "CD117" "CD11b" "CD4" ... #>  $ IQRref    :List of 1 #>   ..$ : num [1:7, 1:25] 0.5 0.5 0.5 0.5 0.5 0.5 0.5 0.5 0.5 0.5 ... #>   .. ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:7] "5" "1" "7" "4" ... #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:25] "CD19" "CD117" "CD11b" "CD4" ... #>  $ MEM_matrix:List of 1 #>   ..$ : num [1:7, 1:25] 3.3093 0.0144 0.0136 3.3341 -0.0029 ... #>   .. ..- attr(*, "dimnames")=List of 2 #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:7] "5" "1" "7" "4" ... #>   .. .. ..$ : chr [1:25] "CD19" "CD117" "CD11b" "CD4" ... #>  $ File Order:List of 1 #>   ..$ : num 0 mem_heatmap(res) mem_heatmap(res, show_mem_labels = TRUE, max_label_markers = 3, min_heatmap_score = 2) mem_labels(res, min_label_score = 1, max_label_markers = 5, show_label_scores = FALSE) #>                                                   5  #>           "5 : ▲ HLADR IgM CD19 CD20 ▼ CD4 CD3 CD8"  #>                                                   1  #>    "1 : ▲ CD3 CD4 CD44 ▼ CD16 CD8 HLADR CD11c CD69"  #>                                                   7  #> "7 : ▲ CD16 HLADR CD56 CD11c CD45RA ▼ CD4 CD3 CD44"  #>                                                   4  #>        "4 : ▲ HLADR CD19 CD20 CD45RA ▼ CD4 CD3 CD8"  #>                                                   2  #>     "2 : ▲ CD8 CD3 CD69 HLADR CD20 ▼ CD4 CD38 CD44"  #>                                                   3  #>   "3 : ▲ CD11c HLADR CD123 CD16 CD33 ▼ CD3 CD4 CD8"  #>                                                   6  #>    "6 : ▲ CD11c CD33 CD61 CD14 CD11b ▼ CD3 CD4 CD8"

title: Introduction to altmem
text: comparison, can run analysis using original cytoMEM package. expected, initial MEM() output altmem(). can also generate cytoMEM heatmap.
code: cytomem_res <- cytoMEM::MEM(PBMC, transform = TRUE, cofactor = 15) identical(res, cytomem_res) #> [1] TRUE cytoMEM::build_heatmaps(cytomem_res, only.MEMheatmap = TRUE) #> [1] "MEM label for cluster 2 : ▲ CD8+7 CD3+4 CD69+2 CD20+1 CD45RA+1 HLADR+1 ▼ CD4-10 CD38-2 CD44-1"                                          #> [2] "MEM label for cluster 6 : ▲ CD33+5 CD11c+5 CD61+4 CD14+4 CD11b+3 CD44+3 HLADR+3 CD123+1 CD38+1 ▼ CD3-7 CD4-5 CD8-3"                     #> [3] "MEM label for cluster 3 : ▲ CD11c+4 HLADR+4 CD123+2 CD45RA+1 CD33+1 CD16+1 CD44+1 ▼ CD3-8 CD4-3 CD8-3 CD11b-1"                          #> [4] "MEM label for cluster 7 : ▲ CD16+6 CD11b+1 CD45RA+1 CD11c+1 CD38+1 HLADR+1 CD56+1 ▼ CD4-10 CD3-6 CD44-2 CD45-1"                         #> [5] "MEM label for cluster 5 : ▲ HLADR+5 IgM+4 CD19+3 CD20+3 CD45RA+1 ▼ CD4-10 CD3-8 CD8-4 CD11b-1 CD69-1 CD44-1"                            #> [6] "MEM label for cluster 4 : ▲ HLADR+4 CD19+3 CD20+3 CD45RA+1 CD44+1 ▼ CD4-9 CD3-8 CD8-3 CD11b-1"                                          #> [7] "MEM label for cluster 1 : ▲ CD4+4 CD3+4 CD44+2 CD61+1 CD45+1 CD33+1 ▼ CD16-9 CD8-7 CD11c-5 HLADR-5 CD69-3 CD11b-2 CD20-2 CD38-1 CD56-1" cytoMEM::build_heatmaps(cytomem_res, display.thresh = 2, labels = TRUE, only.MEMheatmap = TRUE) #> [1] "MEM label for cluster 2 : ▲ CD8+7 CD3+4 CD69+2 ▼ CD4-10 CD38-2"                                      #> [2] "MEM label for cluster 6 : ▲ CD33+5 CD11c+5 CD61+4 CD14+4 CD11b+3 CD44+3 HLADR+3 ▼ CD3-7 CD4-5 CD8-3" #> [3] "MEM label for cluster 3 : ▲ CD11c+4 HLADR+4 CD123+2 ▼ CD3-8 CD4-3 CD8-3"                             #> [4] "MEM label for cluster 7 : ▲ CD16+6 ▼ CD4-10 CD3-6 CD44-2"                                            #> [5] "MEM label for cluster 5 : ▲ HLADR+5 IgM+4 CD19+3 CD20+3 ▼ CD4-10 CD3-8 CD8-4"                        #> [6] "MEM label for cluster 4 : ▲ HLADR+4 CD19+3 CD20+3 ▼ CD4-9 CD3-8 CD8-3"                               #> [7] "MEM label for cluster 1 : ▲ CD4+4 CD3+4 CD44+2 ▼ CD16-9 CD8-7 CD11c-5 HLADR-5 CD69-3 CD11b-2 CD20-2"

title: Authors and Citation
text: Igor Dolgalev. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Dolgalev (2026). altmem: Alternative Interface cytoMEM Marker Enrichment Modeling. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://igordot.github.io/altmem/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {altmem: Alternative Interface to cytoMEM Marker Enrichment Modeling},   author = {Igor Dolgalev},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://igordot.github.io/altmem/}, }

what: Alternative Interface to cytoMEM Marker Enrichment Modeling
title: Alternative Interface to cytoMEM Marker Enrichment Modeling
text: altmem provides alternative interface cytoMEM marker enrichment modeling (MEM). enrichment scores calculated using cytoMEM, inputs outputs adjusted flexibility. Modifications original cytoMEM implementation: Accepts multiple common data structures (data frame, SummarizedExperiment, SingleCellExperiment) Supports non-numeric cluster labels Generates heatmaps MEM label annotations marker filtering Returns MEM labels named vector configurable score marker cutoffs Check documentation website information.

what: altmem: Alternative Interface to cytoMEM Marker Enrichment Modeling — altmem-package
title: altmem: Alternative Interface to cytoMEM Marker Enrichment Modeling — altmem-package
text: Automatically generate display quantitative labels cell populations identified flow cytometry, mass cytometry, single-cell RNA-seq data.

title: altmem: Alternative Interface to cytoMEM Marker Enrichment Modeling — altmem-package
text: Maintainer: Igor Dolgalev igor.dolgalev@nyumc.org

title: Marker Enrichment Modeling — altmem
text: wrapper around cytoMEM::MEM() accepts matrix, data frame, SummarizedExperiment, SingleCellExperiment, supports non-numeric cluster labels.

title: Marker Enrichment Modeling — altmem
code: altmem(   x,   cluster_col,   assay_name = "exprs",   markers = "all",   transform = FALSE,   cofactor = 1,   choose.markers = FALSE,   choose.ref = FALSE,   zero.ref = FALSE,   IQR.thresh = NULL )

title: Marker Enrichment Modeling — altmem
text: x matrix, data frame, SummarizedExperiment, SingleCellExperiment. cluster_col Name column containing cluster labels. assay_name Name assay use expression matrix. used SummarizedExperiment input. Defaults "exprs". markers Character vector marker names include, "" use markers. transform, cofactor, choose.markers, choose.ref, zero.ref, IQR.thresh Passed cytoMEM::MEM().

title: Marker Enrichment Modeling — altmem
text: list matrices (return value cytoMEM::MEM()).

title: Heatmap of MEM scores — mem_heatmap
text: Visualizes MEM enrichment scores heatmap.

title: Heatmap of MEM scores — mem_heatmap
code: mem_heatmap(   x,   show_mem_labels = FALSE,   min_label_score = 1,   max_label_markers = 5,   show_label_scores = FALSE,   min_heatmap_score = 0,   filename = NULL,   width = 15,   height = 8,   cluster_rows = TRUE,   cluster_columns = TRUE,   ... )

title: Heatmap of MEM scores — mem_heatmap
text: x return value altmem() cytoMEM::MEM(). show_mem_labels Whether annotate rows MEM labels. TRUE, labels generated via mem_labels() using min_label_score, max_label_markers, show_label_scores. Defaults FALSE. min_label_score Minimum absolute MEM score marker included label. Defaults 1. max_label_markers Maximum number markers include per direction (positive negative) label. Defaults 5. show_label_scores Whether include MEM scores alongside marker names MEM labels. Defaults FALSE. min_heatmap_score Minimum absolute MEM score across cluster marker shown column heatmap. Increase remove low variance markers. Defaults 0 (show markers). filename Optional file path save heatmap. format inferred extension (e.g., ".png", ".pdf", ".svg"). Defaults NULL (file saved). width, height Plot dimensions inches. used filename provided. cluster_rows Whether cluster rows. Defaults TRUE. cluster_columns Whether cluster columns. Defaults TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap().

title: Heatmap of MEM scores — mem_heatmap
text: ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap object, invisibly.

title: MEM-enriched cluster labels — mem_labels
text: Generates descriptive labels cluster include measured features specifically enriched population based MEM scores, following cytoMEM format. Markers ranked absolute MEM score separated positively negatively enriched groups.

title: MEM-enriched cluster labels — mem_labels
code: mem_labels(   x,   min_label_score = 1,   max_label_markers = 5,   show_label_scores = TRUE )

title: MEM-enriched cluster labels — mem_labels
text: x return value altmem() cytoMEM::MEM(). min_label_score Minimum absolute MEM score marker included label. Defaults 1. max_label_markers Maximum number markers include per direction (positive negative) label. Defaults 5. show_label_scores Whether include MEM scores alongside marker names. Defaults TRUE.

title: MEM-enriched cluster labels — mem_labels
text: named character vector.
